questions.body: If 3x + 3y – 1, 4x² + y – 5, 4x + 2y are the sides of an equilateral triangle, its area is closest to the integer
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: side of an equilateral triangle are 3x + 3y - 1, 4x² + y - 5, 4x + 2y 4x + 2y = 3x + 2y - 1 y = x + 1 4x² + y - 5 = 4x + 2y 4x² - 4x - y = 5 4x² - 4x - x - 1 = 5 4x² - 5x - 6 = 0 x = 2, x = -3/4 (not possible) so x = 2, y = 3 side of triangle (a) = 14 Area of triangle = (sqrt(3)/4) a² = (sqrt(3)/4) × 14² = 84.868 it is nearer to 85

questions.body: The percentage change in acceleration due to gravity at an altitude equal to radius of earth compared to that on the surface of earth is given by
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: On Surface of earth gₑ = GM/R² At heating R, gᴿ = GM/(R + R)² (∴h = R) gᴿ = GM/4R² Δg = GM/4R² - GM/R² Δg = gₑ(1/4 - 1) = -3gₑ/4 Percentage change in g is Δg/gₑ ×100 = -3/4 ×100 % = - 75% (decreased by 75%)

questions.body: Let a, b be two positive real number such that a < b < 1/a and let x = (a + 1/a) - (b + 1/b). Then

choices.a: x is always greater than zero
choices.b: x is always less than zero
choices.d: No such definite conclusion can be drawn about x

questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
given condition is possible only when 0 < a < 1

take a = 1/4 and b = 3 then 1/a = 4
which satisfy the condition
a < b < 1/a

x = a + 1/a - (b + 1/b)
x = a + 1/a - b - 1/b
x = 4 + 1/4 - 3 - 1/3
x = 1 - 1/12
x > 0

questions.body: Let m be the number of distinct (non congruent) integer-sided triangles each with perimeter 15 and n be the number of distinct (non congruent) integer sided triangles each with perimeter 16. Then m - n equals
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
No. of integer sided triangle with perimeter n is given
closest integer to n^2/48 , n is even
closest integer to (n+3)^2/48 , n is odd
m = (15+3)^2/48 = 324/48 = 7
closest integer = 7
n = 16^2/48 = 256/48
closest integer = 5
m - n = 7 - 5 = 2

questions.body: What is the molality of a solution made by dissolving 100 g of bromothymol blue (C27H28Br2O5S) in 1.00 L. of ethanol on a winter's day at a 10°C? The density of ethanol at this temperature is 0.7979 kg L^-1.
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution:
Density (d) = mass/volume = 0.7979 kg
molecule mass of solute
= 27 × 12 + 28 + 2 × 80 + 5 × 16 + 32
= 624
No. of moles of solute
= 100/624 = 0.16025 moles
Molality (m)
= No. of moles of solute/mass of solvent in kg
= 0.16025/0.7979 = 0.201 mol kg^-1

questions.body: Two bulbs of specifications 50W, 220 V and a 100W, 200V are connected first (i)in in parallel and then (ii) In series across 220 V power supply. Choose the correct statement

choices.b: In (i) 50 W will glow brighter and (ii) both will glow equally brighter.
choices.c: In (i) 100 W will glow brighter and (ii) 50 W will glow brighter
choices.d: In both cases the 50 W bulb will glow brighter.

questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
∵ R₆₀ > R₁₀₀
(I) So in Parallel P = V²/R ⇒ P ∝ 1/R
so 100W bulb will glow more
(II) in series P = I²R ⇒ P ∝ R
so 50 W bulb will glow more.

questions.body: At what time (to the nearest second) immediately after 4 O' clock will angle between the hands of the clock be the same as that at 4 O' clock?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
At 4 o' clock angle between min and hour hand = 120°
let at 4 hours × min angle is 120°
so angle is 11/2 x° - 30° × 4 = 120°
x = 480/11
= 43 7/11 min = 43 min 38 sec

questions.body: 0.5755 g of a compound, containing sulfur and fluorine only, has a volume of 255.0 mL at 288.0 K and 50.01 kPa. What is the molecular formula of this compound?
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution:
Following
PV = nRT
PV = mRT/M
P = 50.01 KP₁
= 50.01 × 10³ Pa
= 50.01×10³/1.01325×10⁵ atm
V = 255 mL
V = 255/1000 L
T = 228 K
R = 0.0821 L atm K⁻¹ Mol⁻¹
m = 0.5755 g
∴ M = mRT/PV
M = 1.01325×10⁵×1000×0.5755×0.0821×228/50.01×10³×225
= 108 g
∴ The molecular formula of compound is SF₄

questions.body: If a and b are two positive real numbers such that (a^2 + b^2)/ab = 6, then a positive value of a/b lines between
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
a,b are two positive real numbers
a²+b²/ab = 6
⇒ a/b + b/a = 6
Let x = a/b
x + 1/x = 6
⇒ x² - 6x + 1 = 0
x = 6±√32/2×1
x = 6±4√2/2
x = 3±2√2
x = 3+2√2 or x = 3-2√2
x = 3+2×1.41
or x = 3-2×1.414
x = 3+2.82 or x = 3-2.828
x = 5.82 or x = 0.172
(not in option)
So x = a/b lines between 5 and 6

questions.body: On a field trip in North America, students noticed that when threatened, Horned lizards (Genus : phrynosoma) squirt blood at the attackers. When the professor asked what could have been the reason behind such behaviour of Horned lizards, one student said that certain sensory receptors had fired and triggered a neuronal reflex culminating in increasing the pressure in their sinus cavities until the blood vessels in the corners of the eyes burst. Another student said that it was just an act to frighten off the predator. Thus it can be said that

choices.a: The first response is correct, while the second is incorrect
choices.b: Both explanations are reasonable and can be scientifically tested.
choices.c: The first response is biological, while the second is philosophical.
choices.d: The first explanation is testable as a scientific hypothesis, while the second is not.

questions.subject: biology
questions.solution: (b) Both the explanation for the squirt of horned lizard are correct.

questions.body: The number of real values of a for which the cubic equation x³ - 3ax² + 3ax - a = 0 has all real roots, one of which is a itself, is
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
x³ − 3ax² + 3ax − a = 0
a³ − 3a³ + 3a² − a = 0
− 2a³ + 3a² − a = 0
a(2a² − 3a + 1) = 0
a = 0 or 2a² − 2a − a + 1 = 0
2a (a − 1) − 1 (a − 1) = 0
(a − 1) (2a − 1) = 0
a = 0, 1, 1/2
number of value of a is 2 as at a = 1/2 equation does not have all roots real.

Molecular formula = C₅H₈
Isomers

H₃C − CH = CH − CH₃
H₃C − C = CH₂
     |
CH₃        CH₃
Total 5 isomers

Ex = hf − ϕ
On comparing this with y = mx + c
Slope m = h & intercept c = −ϕ

questions.body: Around a lawn which is of semicircular shape a pavement of uniform width is laid. If the ratio of the area of the lawn to the area of the pavement is 25:24, then the ratio of the outer and inner perimeters of the pavement is

⇒ r₁/r₂ = 7/8
perimeter_inner/perimeter_outer = πr₁/πr₂ = r₁/r₂ = 7/8

questions.body: There three bodies A, B and C. Body A when brought closer to B, attract. When body B is brought closer to C they repel. We can then conclude

choices.d: Body C is nuetral while Body A and B should have opposite charge.

questions.subject: physics

questions.body: A pen costs Rs.13 and a note book costs Rs.35 Let m be the maximum number of items that can be bought for Rs.1000 and n be the minimum number of items that can be bought for the same amount. Then m + n is
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
Equation formed is :
13x + 36y = 1000
x ≠ y both should be integer
So, x = 16, y = 23
1st integer value which satisfy the equation next, x = 50, y = 10
So maximum of x + y = 60, ie means 60 max. items can be purchased and minimum of x + y = 38
∴ (x + y) = 38
(x + y)max = 60 = m
∴ m + n = 98

questions.body: In the x-y plane let A be the point (5,0) and L be the line y = x/3. The number of points p on the line L such that triangle OAP is isosceles is (O being the origin)
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
P lies any = x/3 = 50 K = h/3 => h = 3K
P (3K, K)
Now ΔOAP is isosceles if
(i) OP = PA
(ii) PA = OA
(iii) OA = OP
Case (i) OP = PA => OP₁ = PA²
(3K - 0)² + (K - 0)²
= (3K - 5)² + (K - 0)²
9k² + k² = 9k² - 30k + 25 + k²
=> 30 K = 25
K = 25/30 => K = 5/6 so P(5/2, 5/6)
Case (ii)
PA = OA
PA² = OA²
(3K - 5)² + (K - 0)² = 5²
=> 9K² - 30K + 25 + K² = 25
=> 10K² - 30K = 0
=> 30 K (K - 3) = 0
K = 0/ K = 3
P (0, 0) (not possible) P (9, 3)
Case (iii)
OA² = OP²
5² = (3k - 0)² + (K - 0)²
25 = 9k² + k²
=> 10k² = 25
K² = 5/2
K = ±√(5/2)

questions.body: If x^2 = a + 1 and x + (b/x) = a ; then x equals
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
x³ = a + 1     .....(i)
x + b/x = a
x² + b = a
x
x² - ax + b = 0     .....(ii)
(i) - (ii) × x
x³ - a - 1 = 0
x³ - ax² + bx = 0

ax² - bx - a - 1 = 0     .....(iii)
(iii) - (ii) × a
ax² - bx - a - 1 = 0
ax² - a²x + ab = 0
- + -

(a² - b)x = ab + a + 1
x = (ab + a + 1)/(a² - b)

questions.body: A body of mass 2 kg moving in the positive X-direction with a speed 4ms^-1 collides head on with an another body of mass 3kg moving in the negative X-direction with a speed of 1ms^-1. During collision a loud sound is heard and they both start moving together. The sound energy cannot be greater than n
questions.subject: physics

questions.body: Let a, b, c be positive real numbers such that abc = 1, (ab)^a = (bc)^b = (ca)^c = abc. Then x equals
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
given
(ab)^x = (bc)^x = (ca)^x = abc = k (let)
ab = k^1/x
bc = k^1/x
ca = k^1/x
abc = k
now
a^2b^2c^2 = k^(1+1+1)/x
(abc)^2 = k^3/x
k^2 = k^3/x
2 = 3/x + 1
x = 4/5

questions.body: Which radiation is the easiest to shield ?
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: Alpha (due to high mass)

questions.body: Identify the correct order of sequence from exterior to interior.
questions.subject: biology
questions.solution: Cell → Nucleus → Chromosomes → DNA → Protein

questions.body: In nuclear reactor, the electrons and protons are moving in opposite direction across a small hole in 2 second. If number of electrons and protons are 2 × 10^16 each, the current through the hole is given by
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
Current due to electrons I1
= (2×10^18 × 1.6×10^-19)/2
Current due to protons I2
= (2×10^18 × 1.6×10^-19)/2
So total current
I = I1 + I2 = 2 × 10^18 × 1.6 × 10^-19
= 3.2 × 10^-1
= 3.2 mA

questions.body: Which species below has the same general shape as NH3 ?
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution:
O-S=O → sp^3
O-
Due to presence of lp shape is triangular pyramidal like NH3.
CO3^2-, NO3^-, SO3 all are sp^2 hybridised.

questions.body: After hearing to an influential lecture on "how to conserve environment by avoiding usage of plastic"? Ghan Shyam resolved that he should also contribute towards protecting the environment from plastic menace. Can you suggest him the first step how should he go about doing this effectively?

choices.a: He should urge his parents to stop using plastic materials at home.
choices.b: He should write a letter to the local civic body against selling plastic materials around his locality.
choices.c: He should practice minimizing plastic usage himself.
choices.d: He should ask his teacher to advice people on his behalf to stop usage of plastics.

questions.subject: biology
questions.solution: To conserved the environment person should first minimize the use of plastics.

The comments on the direction and strength of magnetic field are given in column II. Match the following.
questions.subject: physics

questions.body: If the radius of the base of a cone is doubled then the slant are becomes 3 times the original slant area. Suppose when the radius of the base of the cone is quadrupled (that is increased to 4 times), the slant area becomes k times the original slant area. Then the integer closest to k is
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
Let radius, height and slant height be r, h and l
So, slant area of original cone
= πrl
Δ^TQ π2r
sqrt(h^2 + 4r^2) = 3πrsqrt(h^2 + r^2)
⇒ 4 (h^2 + 4r^2) = 9(h^2 + r^2)
⇒ 4h^2 + 16r^2 = 9h^2 + 9r^2
⇒ 7r^2 = 5h^2
7r^2/5 = h^2
Now π4r
sqrt(h^2 + 16r^2) = K or sqrt(h^2 + r^2)
16 (h^2 + 16r^2) = K^2 (h^2 + r^2)
put h^2 = 7r^2/5

⇒ 16 × [7r^2/5 + 16r^2] = K^2 [7r^2/5 + r^2]
= 16 × 87r^2/5 = K^2 12r^2/5
⇒ 16 × 87/12 = K^2
⇒ sqrt(116)
integer closest to K is 11

questions.body: The mass of 0.2 mole of Oxygen molecule is
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution:
Mole = Given mass / Gram molecular mass
0.2 = 6.4/32
W = 6.4 g
